purpose: OpenAI Codex CLI counterpart to ClaudeCodeService ‚Äî spawns codex worker processes for swarm tasks. Builds Codex-specific args (`exec --json` or `exec resume --json`, `--full-auto` or `--dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox`, `--skip-git-repo-check`, `-C projectDir`), passes the prompt via stdin, and parses NDJSON output line-by-line. Writes Codex's `last message` to a temp file (`-o /tmp/synapse_codex_last_message_*.txt`) which is read on close and included in the `worker-complete` payload as `lastMessage`. Strips Codex's noisy `Reading prompt from stdin...` line from stderr.
gotchas:
Unlike Claude, the system prompt is not a CLI flag ‚Äî it is prepended into the prompt text itself ('Follow these operating instructions exactly:
<systemPrompt>

User request:
<prompt>'). Bypass this and the worker will ignore worker-protocol instructions.

`--dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox` is the Codex equivalent of Claude's `--dangerously-skip-permissions`; without it Codex defaults to `--full-auto`. There is no permission-relay path equivalent to Claude's stdio mode.

The temp file `outputLastMessagePath` is created via Date.now() + Math.random() ‚Äî very low collision risk, but if multiple workers spawn within the same ms and Math.random() collides the second worker's lastMessage will be lost.
Codex resume uses `exec resume --json <sessionId>` and intentionally omits `-C projectDir` because Codex stores the project association in its own session metadata.
`cleanErrorOutput` filters the literal string 'Reading prompt from stdin...' ‚Äî if Codex changes that wording the noise will reappear in the UI.
patterns: Mirrors ClaudeCodeService's PID-keyed activeWorkers + 3-channel broadcast pattern (`worker-output`, `worker-complete`, `worker-error`) so callers can swap providers transparently.
lastMessage roundtrip via tmpfile because Codex doesn't surface the final assistant message in NDJSON the way Claude does.
Provider field on every event payload (`provider: 'codex'`) lets the renderer distinguish events when both providers run side-by-side.
conventions: Same `[CodexService]` log prefix convention as the Claude service.
All three error/exit code paths converge on delete-from-activeWorkers + broadcast ‚Äî no leaks even on spawn error.
Same `opts.dangerouslySkipPermissions` boolean used for both providers ‚Äî the service maps it internally to the provider-appropriate flag.
